creator: rv@fore.com
text:
Hi Sheldon,

I talked to the xterm maintainer, (dickey@clark.net), and here is a
transcript of the conversation.

[original mail from dickey]

>  -- using template mhl.format --
> Date:    Wed, 09 Dec 1998 08:34:49 EST
> To:      rv@fore.com
> cc:      dickey@clark.net
> 
> From:    dickey@clark.net
> Subject: http://x13.dejanews.com/getdoc.xp?AN=420294246&CONTEXT=913210259.17247
> 
> Yes - that's a documented feature of xterm, for vt100 compatibility
> (the answerback sequence).  Not a bug (though I should make the value
> a resource).
> 
> >   ports/9020: echoing ^E in an xterm causes it to print 'xterm'  fewer
> >   options

[my reply, and his response]

>  -- using template mhl.format --
> Date:    Wed, 09 Dec 1998 11:42:05 EST
> To:      rv@fore.com
> 
> From:    dickey@clark.net
> Subject: Re: http://x13.dejanews.com/getdoc.xp?AN=420294246&CONTEXT=913210259.1
> > 
> > Is there a way to suppress it? 
> (by way of background, this was a feature that I added sometime in 1996 -
> it just takes a while before people notice and want to amend it).
> 
> -- 
> Thomas E. Dickey
> dickey@clark.net
> http://www.clark.net/pub/dickey
> 

Further to that, he went and added a XResource to suppress that behavior.
I'm not sure which version it was added in, but that might be what you
have. If you are not seeing it, just tack this email along with the problem
report and close it.

Thanks a lot.

rv

 -- using MH template repl.format --
In a previous message, Sheldon Hearn writes:

> 
> Hi Rajesh,
> 
> I've tried to reproduce this unsuccessfully with XFree86-3.3.3.1 and a
> range of shells. I presume you experienced this problem with the
> XFree86-3.3.3 port / package? 
> 
> Could you try to reproduce this with XFree86-3.3.3.1 and feed back? If
> it's still a problem, let us know what your TERM environment variable is
> set to and what version of which shell you use. Output from xterm -v
> would also help.
> 
> Get back to us either way.
> 
> Thanks,
> Sheldon.
>
